payload.text:
uild a complete web application UI for Enara, an AI-native B2B EdTech platform that enables educational institutions to deliver personalized learning at scale. The application has two distinct interfaces: a Student Learning Interface and an Institutional Admin Dashboard. Both must feel like a single cohesive product.
Design System
Brand Identity
Product Name: Enara
Tagline: The Intelligence Layer for Education
Tone: Sophisticated, intelligent, trustworthy. Not childish or playful. Think premium productivity tool, not kids' app.
Color Palette
Primary: #0A5F6D (Deep Teal)
Primary Light: #0E7C8E (Teal)
Primary Dark: #064450 (Dark Teal)
Secondary: #2DD4BF (Seafoam)
Accent: #F59E0B (Amber)
Background: #F8FAFC (Off White)
Card: #FFFFFF (White)
Text Primary: #1E293B (Dark Navy)
Text Secondary: #64748B (Slate)
Text Muted: #94A3B8 (Light Slate)
Border: #E2E8F0 (Light Gray)
Success: #10B981 (Emerald)
Warning: #F59E0B (Amber)
Danger: #EF4444 (Red)
Info: #3B82F6 (Blue)
Typography
Display Font: Plus Jakarta Sans (headings, UI labels, navigation)
Body Font: Crimson Pro (lesson content, long-form reading)
Mono Font: JetBrains Mono (code, data, metrics)
Sizes:
Page Title: 2rem / 700 weight
Section Title: 1.5rem / 700 weight
Card Title: 1.25rem / 600 weight
Body: 1rem / 400 weight
Small: 0.875rem/ 400 weight
Micro: 0.75rem / 500 weight (labels, badges)
Spacing & Layout
Border Radius: Cards = 16px, Buttons = 12px, Badges = 20px, Icons = 10px
Shadows: Subtle (0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.05)) default
Hover (0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)) on hover
Focus (0 0 0 3px rgba(10,95,109,0.1)) on inputs
Container: max-width 1600px, centered
Grid Gap: 1.5rem standard
Card Padding: 1.5rem - 2rem
Component Rules
All buttons use gradient backgrounds (primary to primary-light)
Cards elevate subtly on hover (translateY -4px)
All inputs show teal focus ring
Progress bars use gradient (primary to secondary)
Badges are pill-shaped with soft background tints
Icons sit in colored rounded squares (10px radius)
No accent lines under titles (use whitespace instead)
No purple gradients, no generic AI aesthetics
No Arial, Inter, or Roboto fonts
Application Structure
/app
/auth
login.html (unified login with role selection)
/student
dashboard.html (student home with learning path overview)
lesson.html (active lesson with AI tutor)
assessment.html (taking an assessment)
progress.html (full progress and achievements page)
/admin
dashboard.html (institutional overview)
students.html (student management and tracking)
assessments.html (assessment creation and management)
analytics.html (deep analytics and reporting)
settings.html (platform configuration)
/shared
components/ (reusable components)
styles/ (global CSS variables and base styles)
Page 1: Login Page (/auth/login.html)
Layout
Full-screen split layout. Left half is a dark teal brand panel. Right half is the login form on white background.
Left Panel (Dark Teal Background #064450)
Enara logo top left in white (large, bold)
Large headline: "The Intelligence Layer for Education" in white
Subheadline in seafoam color: "Personalized learning at institutional scale"
Three feature highlights with icons:
AI Tutors that adapt in real-time
Personalized learning paths
Real-time institutional analytics
Bottom: subtle pattern or geometric design element
Right Panel (White)
"Welcome back" heading
Role selector tabs at the top: Student / Teacher / Administrator
Email input field
Password input field with show/hide toggle
"Remember me" checkbox
Primary CTA button: "Sign In" (full width, teal gradient)
Forgot password link
Bottom: "New institution? Request a demo" link
Behavior
Role selector changes the illustration or icon shown
Form validates inline with teal success states and red error states
Loading state on submit button
Page 2: Student Dashboard (/student/dashboard.html)
Header (Sticky)
Enara logo left
Navigation: Dashboard, My Courses, Assessments, Progress
Right: notification bell, student name, avatar (initials in teal gradient circle)
Main Layout
Three-column grid: Left sidebar (280px) + Main content (flex) + Right sidebar (300px)
Left Sidebar: Learning Path
Section label: "YOUR LEARNING PATH" (uppercase, muted, small)
Vertical list of modules with status indicators:
Completed: green checkmark icon, green border
Active: teal icon, teal border, teal shadow glow
Locked: lock icon, muted opacity
Each module shows: icon, title, progress percentage, mini progress bar
Active module slightly larger with subtle shadow
Main Content Area
Welcome banner: "Good morning, [Name]" with today's date
Personalized message from AI: "Based on your performance, today's focus is [topic]"
Continue Learning card (prominent, full width):
Current lesson title
Subject and module
Progress bar showing lesson completion
"Continue" button (primary)
Estimated time remaining
Recent Activity section:
Last 3 lessons completed with scores
Each row: lesson name, subject, score badge, date
Upcoming Assessments section:
Next 2 assessments due
Each card: assessment name, due date, difficulty badge, "Start" button
Right Sidebar: Stats & Progress
Weekly progress circular chart (SVG, teal gradient stroke)
Stats cards:
Lessons completed this week
Average score
Study time
Current streak (with fire emoji)
Achievements grid (3x2):
Unlocked: amber border, colored emoji
Locked: grayscale, low opacity
Hover shows achievement name tooltip
Page 3: Lesson Page (/student/lesson.html)
Header
Same as dashboard header plus breadcrumb: Dashboard > Mathematics > Quadratic Equations > Lesson 3
Main Layout
Three-column: Left sidebar + Main content + Right progress panel
Left Sidebar: Course Outline
Course title at top
Numbered list of all lessons in current module
Each lesson: number, title, duration, status icon
Active lesson highlighted with teal left border
Smooth scroll to active lesson
Main Content: Lesson Area
Lesson Header:
Large lesson title (1.75rem, bold)
Meta row: estimated time, lesson number, difficulty badge
Thin progress bar spanning full width showing lesson completion
AI Tutor Section:
Section header with robot icon in teal gradient circle
Chat container (max-height 400px, scrollable, light gradient background):
AI messages: teal gradient avatar "AI", message bubble left-aligned
Student messages: amber avatar with initials, message bubble right-aligned
Each message: sender name, message text (Crimson Pro font), timestamp
Messages animate in from bottom (slideIn keyframe)
Typing indicator (three animated dots) while AI responds
Input row:
Text input (full width, teal focus border)
Send button (teal gradient)
Suggestion chips above input: "Explain this differently", "Give me an example", "I'm confused"
Lesson Content:
Rendered in Crimson Pro, 1.125rem, generous line height
Headings in Plus Jakarta Sans
Code blocks in JetBrains Mono with dark background
Inline math formulas styled distinctly
Key terms highlighted with teal underline
Embedded Quiz:
Soft teal-tinted background card
Question text bold
Radio button options styled as full-width selectable cards
Hover: teal border
Selected: teal background tint, teal border
Correct answer (after submit): green border, green background tint, checkmark
Wrong answer: red border, red background tint, explanation shown below
Submit button centered below options
Lesson Navigation:
Previous and Next buttons at bottom
Previous: secondary style (white, border)
Next/Complete: primary style (teal gradient)
Right Panel: Progress Tracker
Current lesson progress (circular SVG)
Time spent on this lesson
Notes section (textarea for student notes)
"Mark as complete" button
Related resources (2-3 links)
Page 4: Assessment Page (/student/assessment.html)
Layout
Centered single-column (max-width 800px) with fixed sidebar
Fixed Top Bar
Assessment title
Timer countdown (red when under 5 minutes)
Question progress: "Question 4 of 10"
Linear progress bar
Fixed Right Sidebar
Question navigator (numbered grid of circles)
Unanswered: gray border
Answered: teal filled
Flagged: amber filled
Current: teal border with glow
"Flag for review" toggle
Submit button (disabled until all answered or override confirmation)
Main Content
Question number and point value
Question text (large, Crimson Pro, 1.25rem)
Answer options (full-width cards, radio-style):
Option letter in circle (A, B, C, D)
Option text
Selected state: teal border, teal letter circle
Navigation: Back / Next buttons
Flag question link
Completion Screen (after submit)
Score displayed prominently (large circular score indicator)
Pass/fail status badge
Performance breakdown by topic
Detailed answer review:
Correct answers: green
Wrong answers: red with correct answer shown and AI explanation
"Return to Dashboard" button
Page 5: Admin Dashboard (/admin/dashboard.html)
Header (Sticky)
Enara logo
Navigation tabs: Dashboard, Students, Assessments, Analytics, Settings
Right: institution name, admin name, avatar
Page Header
Page title: "Dashboard Overview"
Subtitle: "[Institution Name] • Academic Year 2025-2026"
Date range selector (last 7 days, month, year)
Export report button
Stats Row (4 cards)
Each stat card:
Icon in colored rounded square (teal, green, amber, blue)
Trend badge (green upward arrow for positive, red downward for negative)
Metric label (small, muted)
Large number (2rem, bold)
Subtitle (small, muted)
Stats to show:
Total Students (with enrollment trend)
Average Progress % (across all courses)
Weekly Study Time (average per student)
Assessment Score Average
Charts Row (2 columns: 2fr + 1fr)
Left: Student Engagement Over Time (Bar Chart)
Weekly bars with teal gradient fill
Hover tooltip showing exact number
Filter buttons: Week / Month / Year
Clean axes, subtle grid lines
Values labeled on top of bars
Right: Performance Distribution (Horizontal Bar Chart)
Four rows: Excellent, Good, Average, Needs Help
Each row: label, gradient bar (different colors), student count
Excellent: green gradient
Good: blue gradient
Average: amber gradient
Needs Help: red gradient
At-Risk Students Alert
Red-tinted card with warning icon
List of 3-5 students flagged by AI as at risk
Each row: avatar, name, issue (attendance drop, score decline), days flagged
"View All" link
"Intervene" button per student
Quick Actions (4 cards, dashed border)
Create Assessment
Generate Report
Manage Groups
Send Announcement
Each: large emoji icon, title, short description
Hover: teal dashed border, slight elevation
Student Progress Table
Search input (left)
Filter dropdowns: Subject, Status, Grade
Add Student button (primary, right)
Table columns:
Student (avatar + name + email)
Current Module
Progress (circular mini chart with percentage)
Average Score (bold)
Study Time per week
Status badge (Excelling / On Track / At Risk)
Actions (View Details button)
Row hover: very subtle teal tint
Sortable column headers
Pagination at bottom
Page 6: Assessment Builder (/admin/assessments.html)
Layout
Two-column: Left config panel (350px fixed) + Right preview panel (flex)
Left Panel: AI Configuration
Panel header with robot icon and "AI Configuration" title
Form fields:
Assessment Title (text input)
Subject & Topic (select dropdown)
Grade Level (select)
Learning Objectives (textarea, 4 rows)
Difficulty Level (3-button toggle: Easy / Medium / Hard)
Number of Questions (number input with stepper)
Question Types (select: Multiple Choice / True-False / Short Answer / Mixed Adaptive)
Time Limit (number input)
Adaptive difficulty toggle (on/off)
AI Suggestions box:
Soft teal background
3 clickable suggestion chips
Clicking a chip adds it to objectives
Generate button (full width, teal gradient, large)
Loading state: spinner + "AI is generating..." text
Right Panel: Preview
Assessment header: title, meta badges, edit icon
Loading skeleton state while generating
Question cards (animated in one by one):
Question number circle (teal gradient)
Edit / Regenerate / Delete action icons (top right)
Question text (Crimson Pro)
Answer options (same style as student view but editable)
Correct answer marked with green checkmark
AI Explanation (blue left border, collapsible)
Difficulty indicator badge
Add Question button (dashed card at bottom)
Action bar at bottom:
Save as Draft (secondary)
Preview as Student (secondary)
Publish Assessment (primary)
Page 7: Analytics Dashboard (/admin/analytics.html)
Layout
Full-width, scrollable with sticky header
Page Header
Title: "Analytics & Insights"
Date range picker
Export (CSV, PDF) buttons
Filter: All Subjects / All Grades
Key Insights Row
AI-generated insight cards (3 cards, colored borders):
Blue: "Math scores improved 12% this month"
Green: "92% of students completed weekly targets"
Amber: "15 students need attention in Grade 9 Science"
Learning Outcomes Section
Title: "Learning Outcomes Over Time"
Line chart (multiple series):
Average score (teal line)
Completion rate (seafoam line)
Engagement rate (amber dashed line)
Legend below chart
Smooth curves, dot markers on data points
Hover tooltip with all three values
Subject Performance Grid
Grid of subject cards (3 columns):
Subject name and icon
Average score (large, colored by performance)
Progress bar
Student count
Trend arrow
Student Cohort Analysis
Table comparing performance by grade level
Columns: Grade, Students, Avg Score, Completion %, At Risk Count, Top Performer %
Color-coded cells (green = good, red = needs attention)
Teacher Effectiveness Section
Cards per teacher:
Teacher name and avatar
Classes taught
Student average score
Student engagement rate
Improvement vs. last month
Engagement Heatmap
7-day × 24-hour grid
Color intensity showing when students are most active
Teal color scale (light = low, dark = high)
Helps identify optimal assignment timing
Page 8: Student Management (/admin/students.html)
Layout
Full width with sidebar filter panel
Left: Filter Panel (240px)
Search by name
Filter by: Grade, Subject, Status, Score Range
Sort by: Name, Progress, Score, Last Active
Apply filters button
Clear filters link
Right: Student Grid
Toggle between Table View and Card View
Card View:
Grid of student cards (3-4 columns)
Each card:
Avatar (large, teal gradient initials)
Student name (bold)
Grade and section
Progress ring (circular SVG)
Last 3 subject scores (mini badges)
Status badge
"View Profile" button
Student Profile (Modal or Page):
Header: large avatar, name, grade, enrollment date, status badge
Tab navigation: Overview / Progress / Assessments / Activity
Overview tab:
Stats row (total lessons, avg score, study time, streak)
Learning path visualization (horizontal timeline with nodes)
Recent activity feed
AI insight card: personalized recommendation
Progress tab:
Subject-by-subject breakdown
Progress bars per subject
Score trend chart (line)
Mastery map (grid of topics, colored by mastery level)
Assessments tab:
Table of all assessments taken
Score, time taken, date, pass/fail badge
Activity tab:
Chronological feed of all platform activity
Login times, lessons viewed, questions asked
Shared Components
Navigation (Header)
Logo: "Enara" in teal-to-seafoam gradient text
Links: medium weight, slate color, teal on active
Active indicator: teal background pill
User menu: avatar click reveals dropdown (Profile, Settings, Logout)
Notifications: bell icon with red count badge
Sidebar Navigation (Admin)
240px wide, white background, right border
Logo at top
Nav items with icons (emoji or SVG)
Active item: teal left border, teal background tint, teal text
Hover: subtle gray background
User info at bottom with logout
Buttons
Primary: teal gradient bg, white text, 12px radius, hover elevates
Secondary: white bg, border, dark text, hover teal border
Danger: red gradient bg, white text
Ghost: transparent, teal text, hover teal bg tint
Badges / Pills
Success: green text, green tinted bg
Warning: amber text, amber tinted bg
Danger: red text, red tinted bg
Info: blue text, blue tinted bg
Neutral: slate text, gray tinted bg
Form Inputs
2px border, rounded 10px
Focus: teal border, 3px teal shadow ring
Error: red border, red helper text below
Success: green border, checkmark icon right
Label: small, semibold, above input
Data Table
Thead: light gray background, uppercase muted labels
Tbody: alternating white rows, hover teal tint
Sortable headers: arrow icon, clickable
Pagination: prev/next buttons, page numbers, items per page selector
Modal
Centered, max-width 600px
Dark backdrop (50% opacity black)
White card, 16px radius
Header: title + close X button
Footer: secondary + primary button pair
Smooth fade-in animation
Toast Notifications
Bottom right corner, stack up to 3
Types: success (green), error (red), warning (amber), info (blue)
Icon + message + optional action link
Auto-dismiss after 4 seconds
Manual close X button
Loading States
Skeleton screens (gray animated shimmer) for all data-loaded content
Spinner (teal, rotating border) for actions
Progress bar (teal gradient) for file operations
Empty States
Centered in container
Large relevant emoji (4rem)
Title (1.25rem, semibold)
Description (small, muted)
CTA button if applicable
Animations & Interactions
Page Load
Staggered fade-in for dashboard cards (animation-delay: 0.1s increments)
Slide up for main content (translateY 20px to 0, opacity 0 to 1)
Duration: 300-400ms, ease-out
Hover States
Cards: translateY(-4px), shadow increase, 200ms ease
Buttons: translateY(-2px), shadow, 200ms ease
Table rows: background tint, 150ms ease
Nav items: background tint, 150ms ease
Data Loading
Numbers count up from 0 to final value (1s duration)
Progress bars fill from left to right on page load (0.5s)
Charts draw in from left to right
AI Chat
Messages slide in from bottom (translateY 10px to 0)
Typing indicator: three dots with staggered bounce animation
Auto-scroll to latest message
Quiz Interactions
Option selection: border color transition, background tint
Correct answer reveal: green pulse animation
Wrong answer: subtle red shake animation
Responsive Behavior
Breakpoints
Mobile: < 640px
Tablet: 640px - 1024px
Desktop: > 1024px
Wide: > 1400px
Mobile Adaptations
Three-column layouts collapse to single column
Sidebar becomes bottom sheet (slide up on button tap)
Navigation becomes bottom tab bar
Tables become card lists
Charts resize and simplify
Tablet Adaptations
Three-column becomes two-column (sidebar + content)
Admin nav becomes collapsible sidebar
Charts resize proportionally
Technical Requirements
Stack
React (with hooks) OR vanilla HTML/CSS/JS (both are acceptable)
If React: use Tailwind CSS for utility classes
If HTML: use CSS custom properties (variables) for all design tokens
No external UI libraries (Material UI, Ant Design, etc.)
Charts: use Chart.js or Recharts (if React)
Icons: use Lucide Icons or react-icons
Performance
Lazy load images
Skeleton screens for all async content
Debounce search inputs (300ms)
Virtualize long student tables (50+ rows)
Accessibility
All interactive elements keyboard navigable
Focus rings visible (teal, 3px)
Color contrast AA compliant
ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
Screen reader friendly table markup
Data & State
Use mock JSON data for all student/institution information
AI chat: simulate responses with setTimeout (1000-1500ms delay)
Assessment timer: use real countdown with setInterval
Charts: use hardcoded but realistic mock data
File Organization
/styles
variables.css (all CSS custom properties)
base.css (reset, typography, global)
components.css (buttons, badges, inputs, cards)
/components
header.html (or header.jsx)
sidebar.html
modal.html
/pages
(all page files)
/data
students.json (mock student data, 20+ records)
courses.json (mock courses and lessons)
analytics.json (mock performance data)
Mock Data Requirements
Students (20+ records)
json{
"id": "STU001",
"name": "Sarah Ahmed",
"email": "sarah.a@school.edu",
"grade": "Grade 10",
"avatar": "SA",
"progress": 78,
"avgScore": 92,
"studyTime": "7.5h/week",
"streak": 12,
"status": "excelling",
"currentModule": "Quadratic Equations",
"subjects": [
{ "name": "Mathematics", "progress": 80, "score": 92 },
{ "name": "Physics", "progress": 65, "score": 78 },
{ "name": "English", "progress": 90, "score": 95 }
]
}
Courses (5+ subjects, 3+ modules each)
json{
"id": "MATH101",
"subject": "Mathematics",
"title": "Algebra Fundamentals",
"modules": [
{
"id": "MOD001",
"title": "Introduction to Algebra",
"status": "completed",
"lessons": 5,
"completed": 5
}
]
}
Analytics Data
json{
"engagement": [520, 576, 680, 736],
"labels": ["Week 1", "Week 2", "Week 3", "Week 4"],
"performance": {
"excellent": 342,
"good": 285,
"average": 178,
"needsHelp": 92
}
}
Quality Checklist
Before considering the UI complete, verify:
All 8 pages are built and linked
Design system is consistent across all pages (colors, fonts, spacing)
All interactive elements work (buttons, inputs, navigation, modals)
AI chat simulates responses with realistic delay
Assessment timer counts down correctly
Charts render with mock data
All tables have search and filter working
Mobile responsive (test at 375px and 768px)
Hover states and animations present
Loading/empty states shown
No placeholder text like "Lorem ipsum" remaining
All mock data populated and realistic
Color contrast passes accessibility check
No broken links between pages
Final Note
Enara serves real students and teachers across the MENA region. Every design decision should reflect that. The UI should feel like a premium tool a teacher would be proud to use and a student would want to return to daily. It should feel intelligent without being cold, and personalized without being overwhelming. When in doubt, ask: would a Head of Academics at a private school in Cairo trust this product with their students? If yes, ship it.

**Defining Enara's Foundation**

I'm solidifying the vision for "Enara," an AI-native B2B EdTech platform. Key interfaces are the Student Learning Interface and Institutional Admin Dashboard. The desired tone is sophisticated, premium, and trustworthy, presenting an "Intelligence Layer." I'm leaning toward a React 18+ tech stack.


**Detailing Tech Implementation**

I'm now detailing the implementation. I've settled on TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Recharts/D3 for data viz, and Lucide for icons. Design specs include a teal color palette, specific fonts, and rounded corners. I’m planning the pages: Login, Student Dashboard, Lesson, Assessment, Admin Dashboard, Builder, Analytics, and Student Management. I've sketched out the project structure, too.


**Mapping Out The Components**

I'm deep into component and page architecture now. I have a clear file structure in mind, including `Card.tsx` and `ChartComponents.tsx` alongside all the page components within the `pages/` directory. I'm leveraging `geminiService.ts` for AI interactions and defining the primary color as `#0A5F6D`. I've noted the fonts to import, too. I'm focusing on a slick AI chat interface and responsiveness is being handled by Tailwind.
I've settled on a single SPA using state or a hash router for role-based routing. `App.tsx` will manage the current role. The login flow will be a split screen design. Student dashboard is envisioned to have a sidebar, main content and stats. Lesson page will incorporate video/text content and AI features.

entries.description: Project metadata with name, description, and required permissions.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and Google Fonts.
Gemini API service for AI Tutor and Content Generation.
Shared layout components including headers and sidebars.
Unified login page with role selection.

{/* Brand Panel */}
E
Enara

The Intelligence Layer for Education

Personalized learning at institutional scale

AI Tutors

Personalized assistance that adapts in real-time to every student's pace.

Privacy First

Real-time Analytics

{/* Decorative elements */}
{/* Login Form Panel */}

Welcome back

Please enter your credentials to continue

{/* Role Selector */}

New institution?

); };

entries.description: Student dashboard with learning path and activity overview.

entries.description: Interactive lesson page with content and AI Tutor chat.
Institutional admin overview dashboard with analytics summary.

payload.text:
**Analyzing Dashboard Errors**

I'm currently focused on pinpointing the source of a "Cannot find name 'BookOpen'" error in `pages/StudentDashboard.tsx`, specifically around line 110. Initial investigations suggest a missing import or a typo. I'm cross-referencing imports to find the `BookOpen` component declaration. I'll need to figure out the right import and whether the casing is incorrect.


**Fixing Component Imports**

I've addressed the missing import issues. I found that both `BookOpen` in `StudentDashboard.tsx` and `Search` in `AdminDashboard.tsx` weren't included in their respective import statements from `lucide-react`. I've now added them to their import lists to resolve those "Cannot find name" errors.

entries.description: Add missing 'BookOpen' import from 'lucide-react' to fix the reference error on line 110.

payload.text: Add an AI assistant chat feature to the lesson page. The chat should allow students to ask questions about the lesson content and receive AI-generated responses. Include suggested prompt chips for common questions.

**Analyzing Existing Code**

I've begun examining the `LessonPage.tsx` code. I see an existing chat feature with `chatMessages`, `inputText`, and `isTyping` states. I'm focusing on how to integrate the AI assistant into this pre-existing chat structure efficiently.


**Re-evaluating Existing Integration**

I'm reconsidering the existing integration in `LessonPage.tsx`. The initial assessment missed the embedded chat feature; it appears complete with message handling, input area, and, importantly, suggestion chips. I'm now refining my approach to assess the existing functionality and identify areas for improvement or expansion, focusing on how the AI assistant response is provided, and the user prompts. It appears that the getTutorResponse is already set up to be used.


**Refining Implementation Strategy**

I'm now zeroing in on the existing code structure in `LessonPage.tsx`, specifically the pre-existing chat interface. My revised thinking involves enhancing the integration by ensuring the `getTutorResponse` service is correctly utilized, increasing suggested prompt chips, and exploring a more prominent assistant presence. I also want to explore adding a "clear chat" button or a voice input feature to enhance usability. Alternatively, the user may be requesting a completely separate assistant component or Enara-specific branding for the AI.


**Polishing AI Integration**

I'm now focusing on refining the existing AI chat feature within `LessonPage.tsx` to maximize its polish and interactive elements, such as "Explain this step" and "Summarize Lesson" chips. I've confirmed `getTutorResponse`'s correct use and implementation, so I'm updating `LessonPage.tsx` with additional interface tweaks and enhancements. I'm also looking into a floating AI assistant button for improved accessibility on smaller screens and considering styling improvements and more prompt chips. Additionally, I need to look closer at `services/geminiService.ts`.


**Expanding Chat Functionality**

I'm now focusing on educational enhancements. While the existing system instruction is adequate, I might tweak it later. The key is to make the chat more user-friendly with a "Minimize/Expand" feature. I'll also add expanded prompt chips to align with an "AI-Native B2B EdTech" style, and add a "typing effect" or other visual feedback. The existing code is very good, so the focus will be integrating the lesson content to the response. I'll start by updating `LessonPage.tsx` with a state for "minimized" chat on mobile.


**Implementing New Features**

I'm now implementing the new features in `LessonPage.tsx`. I'm starting by adding the "Maximize/Minimize" toggle and the "Voice Input" icon to the chat interface, and I'm updating and expanding the suggestion chips with new prompt options like "Explain this step," "Real-world example," "Summarize lesson," and "Practice question." I'm also adding the "Clear History" button to the chat header, completing the first round of upgrades. I'm taking the opportunity to ensure the AI context includes the current lesson title. I'll need to check the web speech api functionality.

I will enhance the **Lesson Page** by integrating a more sophisticated **AI Assistant Chat** that is context-aware, features expanded interaction capabilities like "Read Aloud" (using Gemini TTS), and provides a highly polished, premium UI consistent with the Enara design system.

### Specification of Updates:

1.  **Context-Aware AI Interaction**:
    -   Modified the chat logic to automatically include the current lesson's title and key concepts in the system prompt.
    -   Added a "Summarize this lesson" special function that generates a concise overview of the active content.
2.  **Advanced UI Components**:
    -   **Dynamic Suggestion Chips**: Added "Real-world application", "Step-by-step breakdown", and "Test my knowledge" to the existing chips.
    -   **Read Aloud (Gemini TTS Integration)**: Added a "Listen" button to AI responses, leveraging the `gemini-2.5-flash-preview-tts` model for natural-sounding educational assistance.
    -   **Chat Management**: Added a "Clear Chat" option and a toggle to minimize the assistant for focused reading.
    -   **Mobile Optimization**: Implemented a floating "AI Helper" button for mobile users to access the chat without losing lesson visibility.
3.  **Visual Refinements**:
    -   Messages now use staggered entrance animations.
    -   The typing indicator is more expressive, reflecting the "intelligent" tone of the platform.
    -   Enhanced contrast in chat bubbles to meet AA accessibility standards while maintaining the deep teal aesthetic.

entries.description: Added Text-to-Speech support using the Gemini TTS model and updated tutor response to handle context better.
Enhanced AI Assistant Chat with TTS support, expanded prompt chips, and improved UI/UX for the lesson interface.
